feature.title: Project stories, team milestones, and field updates.
feature.summary: This featured slot is ready for the lead story on the page. Use it for a major announcement, project spotlight, or anything you want visitors to notice first.
feature.imageAlt: Construction surveying team on an active project site
feature.author: MG Vera Team
body: This featured story area is designed for the kind of update visitors should see first: a major project milestone, a company announcement, or a field story that shows the work behind the deliverable.
As the news page grows, this article can become a more detailed recap with project context, team notes, and images that help clients understand how MG Vera approaches complex work across Florida.
Use this page for the full story, while the news landing page can stay focused on quick scanning and discovery.

articles.title: Survey field notes can become a short project recap here.
articles.summary: Use this card for a recent site visit, completed deliverable, or a milestone worth sharing with clients and partners.
articles.imageAlt: LiDAR scanning equipment capturing site conditions
articles.author: MG Vera Team
body: Field notes are often where the story of a project starts. This article space can turn a recent site visit, scan, or completed deliverable into a short recap for clients and partners.
The goal is to keep the update practical: what was completed, why it mattered, and how the team approached the work in the field.
Over time, these recaps can become a useful record of the range of environments, scopes, and technical challenges MG Vera supports.

articles.title: Team announcements and internal wins can live in this stream.
articles.summary: This could become a space for hiring updates, certifications, anniversaries, or recognitions across the company.
articles.imageAlt: MG Vera team and family gathering
articles.author: MG Vera Team
body: Company updates give visitors a look at the people and momentum behind the work. This post can be used for hiring news, certifications, anniversaries, and recognitions.
A strong update does not need to be long. A few clear paragraphs can explain what happened, who was involved, and why it matters for the firm or its clients.
These stories help make the news page feel active while keeping the focus on the company culture that supports the technical work.

articles.title: A third story card gives you room for regular publishing rhythm.
articles.summary: Keep this section flexible for quick notes, press mentions, or a rotating list of recent news items as the page grows.
articles.imageAlt: Surveying and mapping team working in the field
articles.author: MG Vera Team
body: This article template is flexible enough for quick field notes, press mentions, and short updates that should have a permanent place on the site.
Regular publishing does not have to mean long-form writing every time. Short, useful updates can show consistency and give visitors a reason to return.
As the archive grows, the news page can become a timeline of projects, milestones, and team activity.

articles.title: Awards, certifications, and new capabilities can be added as short stories.
articles.summary: This item works well for quick wins that still deserve a permanent place on the site and are easy for visitors to scan.
articles.imageAlt: Utility coordination field work and infrastructure review
articles.author: MG Vera Team
body: Recognition posts are useful for awards, certifications, new service capabilities, and other milestones that clients may want to know about.
This format keeps the story easy to scan while still giving enough detail to explain the achievement and its practical value.
When these updates accumulate, they help communicate steady progress without turning the page into a dense press release archive.

articles.title: Milestones with partners can become another kind of article entry.
articles.summary: Use this card to highlight project phases, partnership announcements, or meaningful work completed with clients across Florida.
articles.author: MG Vera Team
body: Partner milestones can become another useful category for the news archive. These updates can highlight project phases, collaboration, or meaningful work completed with clients.
The article page gives the story room to explain the context while the landing page keeps the item easy to discover.
This is also a good place to connect present work back to the long relationships and history that shape MG Vera.

articles.title: The archive can grow into a fuller timeline of what the company has been doing.
articles.summary: As you write more articles, this layout can become a simple backlog of stories that visitors can progressively reveal.
articles.imageAlt: Residential and commercial surveying at a developed property
articles.author: MG Vera Team
body: The archive can grow into a fuller timeline of what the company has been doing, from project work to internal milestones and community presence.
This format gives each story a dedicated place without requiring the news landing page to carry every detail at once.
As more stories are added, the detail pages can become a lightweight but polished publishing system for MG Vera.
